title: phacelurus digitatus
intro: Phacelurus digitatus, also known as the African or Indian club moss, is a type of vascular plant that belongs to the family Lycopodiaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Phacelurus digitatus include reducing inflammation, improving respiratory function, and acting as an antioxidant. Herbal preparations of Phacelurus digitatus are typically made by drying the leaves and stems, which can be used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures for medicinal purposes.
